linux-gfx90a.blocked_reason: PORTED. Builds clean for gfx90a (USE_HIP). GPU gate: cvcuda_test_system FULLY GREEN (0 failures, exit 0, 2608 pass, re-confirmed this cycle) -- the operator-correctness gate. nvcv_test_cudatools_system 1116/1123; the 7 residuals are now ROOT-CAUSED to non-production artifacts (no source change needed this cycle). RESIDUAL A (6-8 InterpolationVarShapeWrap.correct_shift, non-deterministic set): a TEST-FIXTURE use-after-free, NOT a GPU/operator/allocator bug. The test issues cudaMemcpy2DAsync from a per-iteration-local std::vector dstVec that is destroyed while the async copy is pending; CUDA's pageable async copy is effectively synchronous (works), HIP's hipMemcpy2DAsync from pageable memory is truly async so it reads the freed/reused buffer and writes garbage into the dst row-stride padding the kernel does not overwrite -> full-strided compare vs a zeroed ref mismatches, failing set varies run-to-run. PROVEN: an alloc-probe shows the production hipMemset(0) zeroes the FULL padded buffer (postMemsetNZ=0); a pre-kernel probe shows padding dirty BEFORE the kernel and unchanged after (kernel never writes padding); keeping dstVec alive (no sync change) OR syncing after the fill makes all 21 pass 3/3 runs, baseline fails 6-8. Conclusively a test-side artifact (upstream UB exposed by HIP semantics); do not edit the test. RESIDUAL B (1 TypeTraitsMakeTypeVectorTest/3): char-vs-signed-char type identity dictated by the upstream HIP vector header (char4 = HIP_vector_type<char,N>, members `char`; CV-CUDA must set BaseType<charN>=char on HIP so the reference accessor binds). Forcing signed char would need a non-canonical HIP_vector_type<signed char,N> family in shared code (per-platform hack, breaks arithmetic); true upstream-header deferral. 1122/1123 with B excluded. Six prior arch-unified root-cause fixes intact (HEAD 74c53d86): texture-pitch clamp 256->32, HistogramEqVarShape memset, PWM PointT union + wave64 BlockReduce __syncthreads, __fsqrt_rn->f64, cuda::min/max NaN match, -ffp-contract=on. Full per-cluster analysis in notes.md; new fault classes added to PORTING_GUIDE.
